hero-title: Beginner Path: Start Learning About AI Today
Beginner Path: Start Learning Augmented Intelligence Today
Beginner Path: Start Learning Augmented Intelligence Today
hero-lead: This path is for anyone who is curious about AI and how it can help with things you already do. You do not need any tech know-how. You do not need to know any special words. You do not need to have tried it before. Just come with an open mind and a bit of curiosity.

This path is for anyone curious about AI and how it can help with what you already do. You do not need a tech background, any special words, or any past experience. Just bring your curiosity and an open mind.
This path is for anyone curious about AI and how it can amplify what you already do. No technical background needed, no jargon prerequisites, no prior experience required. Just bring your curiosity.
For anyone curious how AI can amplify what you already do. No technical background, jargon, or prior experience required. Bring curiosity.
For the curious. No background, jargon, or experience needed.
what-intro: The claim this path makes is that augmented intelligence is not about swapping yourself out for a machine — it is about learning what AI is good at, what it is bad at, and how to put its strengths together with yours. Whether the two of you can then do things neither could do alone is something you can test as you go.

The claim here is that augmented intelligence is not about replacing yourself with a machine, but about learning what AI does well, what it does poorly, and how to combine its strengths with yours. Whether that lets you do things neither of you could alone is yours to judge.

This path argues that augmented intelligence is not about replacing yourself with a machine — it is about understanding what AI actually does well, what it does poorly, and how to combine its strengths with yours. Whether that combination reaches outcomes neither of you could manage alone is something you can decide for yourself.

The argument: augmented intelligence is not replacement, but knowing what AI does well, what it does poorly, and combining its strengths with yours. Whether that reaches what neither could alone is yours to judge.
The claim: not replacement, but combining AI's strengths with yours. Judge for yourself.
what-outcomes: This path lays out the big idea behind augmented intelligence, the real things today's AI cannot do well yet, and a picture of how people and machines might think together — all for you to weigh up. Whether it convinces you to take the Practitioner Path next is entirely your call.

This path lays out the core idea behind augmented intelligence, the real limits of today's AI, and a picture of how people and machines might think together — for you to weigh up. Whether the Practitioner Path is your next step is yours to decide.

This path lays out the core philosophy of augmented intelligence, the real limitations of current AI systems, and a working mental model for how humans and machines think together — for you to weigh up. Whether it earns the Practitioner Path as your next step is yours to decide.

This path lays out the core philosophy of augmented intelligence, current AI's real limitations, and a working model of how humans and machines think together — for you to weigh. Whether the Practitioner Path follows is your call.
It lays out the core philosophy, current AI's limits, and a human-machine model — for you to weigh. The Practitioner Path is your call.
no-prereqs: You do not need to know anything first. This path starts right at the very beginning and builds up bit by bit. Each part points you to the next one. But if something grabs your interest, you can jump straight to it instead.
No prerequisites. This path starts at the very beginning and builds up step by step. Each module links to the next, but you can also jump straight to whatever interests you most.
No prerequisites. This path starts from zero and builds up. Each module links to the next, but you can also jump to whatever interests you most.
No prerequisites. Starts from zero and builds up. Modules link in sequence, or jump to whatever interests you.
No prerequisites. Builds from zero; read in order or jump.
curriculum-intro: Go through these five modules one after the other, in order. Each one builds on the one before it. Step by step, they give you a deeper and deeper understanding of augmented intelligence.
Work through these five modules in order. Each one builds on the last, so step by step you gain a deeper understanding of augmented intelligence.
Work through these five modules in order. Each one builds on the last, giving you a progressively deeper understanding of augmented intelligence.
Work through the five modules in order; each builds on the last for progressively deeper understanding.
Five modules, in order, each building deeper understanding.
module-1-desc: Start here. You will meet what augmented intelligence is said to mean: boosting what people can do by adding machine power, rather than taking the person out of the picture. You will meet the idea of the digital cyborg, see how artificial intelligence is different from augmented intelligence, and ask whether this one difference changes as much as the path claims.

Start here. You will meet the core idea: augmented intelligence framed as amplifying what people can do with machine power, not replacing the person. You will meet the digital cyborg concept, see how artificial and augmented intelligence differ, and weigh whether that difference reshapes as much as it claims.

Start here. Meet the core definition on offer: augmented intelligence as amplifying human potential with machine capability, rather than replacing the human. You will explore the digital cyborg concept, understand the difference between artificial and augmented intelligence, and decide whether this distinction reshapes anything for you.

Start here. The core definition on offer: augmented intelligence amplifies human potential with machine capability rather than replacing the human. Covers the digital cyborg concept, artificial versus augmented intelligence, and whether the distinction reshapes anything.
Core definition on offer: amplify, not replace. Covers the digital cyborg, artificial versus augmented intelligence, and whether it matters.
module-2-desc: Before you lean on AI, it helps to see where it falls down. This module looks at how AI sometimes makes things up, how very expensive big models are to run, how it does not really reason the way we do, and the gap between what AI companies say it can do and what it truly does. Once you have seen where it breaks, how much you choose to trust it is yours to decide.

Before you rely on AI, it helps to see where it breaks. This module covers how it makes things up (hallucination), the huge cost of running big models, its lack of real reasoning, and the gap between what AI companies promise and what the tech delivers. Once you know these limits, how far to trust it is your call.

Before you depend on AI, it helps to see where it breaks. This module covers hallucination, the staggering costs of running large models, the lack of genuine reasoning, and the gap between what AI companies promise and what their technology actually delivers. Whether knowing these limitations changes how far you trust the tool is yours to decide.

Before depending on AI, see where it breaks: hallucination, the staggering cost of large models, the absence of genuine reasoning, and the gap between company promises and delivery. How far these limits change your trust in the tool is yours to weigh.
AI's failure modes: hallucination, model cost, no real reasoning, promise-versus-delivery gap. How far they change your trust is yours to weigh.
module-3-desc: Now that you have met what augmented intelligence is and where AI gets things wrong, look at the case for people and machines working together. This module looks at how modern work keeps getting more complicated, how there is too much information for one person to handle, and the argument that the future favours people who think alongside machines instead of trying to beat them — see whether it persuades you.

Now that you have met what augmented intelligence is and what AI gets wrong, explore the case for the human-machine partnership. This module looks at the growing complexity of modern work, the problem of too much information, and the argument that the future favours people who think alongside machines rather than compete against them — decide for yourself if it holds.

Now that you have met what augmented intelligence is and what AI gets wrong, explore the case for the human-machine partnership. This module examines the growing complexity of modern work, the information overload problem, and the argument that the future favours people who think alongside machines rather than compete against them — see if it persuades you.

Now you have met what augmented intelligence is and what AI gets wrong, explore the case for the partnership: the growing complexity of modern work, information overload, and the argument that the future favours those who think alongside machines, not against them — yours to judge.
The case for the partnership: rising work complexity, information overload, and the argument that the future favours those who think with machines, not against them — yours to judge.
module-4-desc: Theory is helpful, but real examples make it come alive. This module walks you through everyday situations where augmented intelligence changes how work gets done. It shows you how to speed up research, hand off the boring mechanical parts of creative work, help bigger teams work together, and win back time for the kind of thinking that really needs a human brain.

Theory is useful, but examples make it real. This module walks through real situations where augmented intelligence changes daily work: speeding up research, automating the mechanical parts of creative work, helping teams collaborate at scale, and winning back time for the thinking that truly needs a human brain.

Theory is useful, but examples make it real. This module walks through concrete scenarios where augmented intelligence transforms daily work: compressing research cycles, automating the mechanical parts of creative work, scaling collaboration across teams, and reclaiming time for the thinking that actually requires a human brain.

Examples make theory real. This module covers concrete scenarios where augmented intelligence transforms daily work: compressing research cycles, automating mechanical creative tasks, scaling team collaboration, and reclaiming time for thinking that requires a human brain.
Concrete scenarios: compressed research, automated mechanical creative work, scaled collaboration, time reclaimed for human thinking.
module-5-desc: This module argues that augmented intelligence rests on three pillars. The first is Logic: the scientific method and clear mathematical thinking. The second is Explanation: building solid explanations that are hard to twist or break. The third is Architected Data Objects: tidy, standard structures that take something complicated and pack it down small. Whether these three really hold it all up is for you to test as you go into the Practitioner and Researcher paths.

This module argues that augmented intelligence rests on three pillars: Logic (the scientific method and mathematical reasoning), Explanation (building durable, hard-to-vary explanations), and Architected Data Objects (standard structures that pack complexity down small). Whether these pillars hold up is yours to test across the Practitioner and Researcher paths.

This module argues that augmented intelligence rests on three pillars: Logic (the scientific method and mathematical reasoning), Explanation (creating durable, hard-to-vary explanations), and Architected Data Objects (standardised structures that compress complexity). Whether these pillars really form the foundation is for you to test across the Practitioner and Researcher paths.

Three pillars, this module argues, underpin augmented intelligence: Logic (scientific method and mathematical reasoning), Explanation (durable, hard-to-vary explanations), and Architected Data Objects (standardised structures that compress complexity). Whether they hold is yours to test across the Practitioner and Researcher paths.
Three pillars it argues underpin augmented intelligence: Logic, Explanation, and Architected Data Objects. Whether they hold is yours to test.
next-summary: Once you have gone through all five modules, you can decide for yourself what augmented intelligence is, whether it matters, and whether the set of ideas it is built on holds together. That may be enough to start using these ideas in a loose, everyday way in your own work — or to push back on them.

Once you have worked through all five modules, you can judge for yourself what augmented intelligence is, whether it matters, and whether the framework it rests on holds up. That may be enough to start applying these ideas informally in your own work — or to disagree with them.

Once you have worked through all five modules, you can decide for yourself what augmented intelligence is, whether it matters, and whether the conceptual framework it rests on holds up. That may be enough to start applying these ideas informally in your own work — or to push back on them.

After all five modules, you can judge for yourself what augmented intelligence is, whether it matters, and whether its framework holds — enough to start applying these ideas informally, or to argue with them.
After five modules, judge for yourself whether augmented intelligence and its framework hold — enough to apply informally, or to push back.
next-practitioner: If you want to go further and learn the exact methods, tools, and plans for weaving augmented intelligence into your job, the Practitioner Path is made for just that. It picks up right where this path leaves off and builds on everything you have learned here.

If you want to go deeper and learn specific methods, tools, and strategies for weaving augmented intelligence into your professional work, the Practitioner Path is built for exactly that. It builds directly on everything you have learned here.

If you want to go deeper and learn specific frameworks, tools, and strategies for integrating augmented intelligence into your professional workflow, the Practitioner Path is designed exactly for that. It builds directly on everything you have learned here.

To go deeper into specific frameworks, tools, and strategies for integrating augmented intelligence into your professional workflow, the Practitioner Path is designed for exactly that, building directly on everything learned here.
For frameworks, tools, and strategies to integrate it into your workflow, take the Practitioner Path. It builds on this.
